mainEntity.name: How much does a 368DT (3.6 TDV8) engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 368DT TDV8 typically costs £5,500–£8,000 supply only, varying by specification and rebuild quality. Used units can be found from £3,000–£5,000, but provenance is critical. Fitting adds £1,000–£2,000 depending on labour rates.

mainEntity.name: Is the 368DT (3.6 TDV8) a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 368DT has a poor reputation (3.0★). While it offers strong V8 torque and refinement, the early engines (2006–2009) are known for timing chain tensioner failure and, in more severe cases, crankshaft fracture. It is not the reliable 4.4 TDV8 (448DT) that replaced it.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common 368DT failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner failure is the most critical on early (2006–2009) engines — causing chain slack, timing drift, and catastrophic valve/piston interference. Turbo actuator failure and crankshaft fracture are also well-documented risks.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the 368DT?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 368DT powered the Range Rover L322 TDV8 from 2006 to 2009 and the Range Rover Sport L320 TDV8 from 2006 to 2012.

mainEntity.name: How long does the 368DT (3.6 TDV8) last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With meticulous maintenance and early timing chain tensioner replacement on pre‑2009 examples, 368DT engines can reach 150,000+ miles. However, many fail well before this due to the known issues. Later (2009+) examples are more durable but still require careful maintenance.

items.question: How much does a 368DT (3.6 TDV8) engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned 368DT TDV8 typically costs £5,500–£8,000 supply only, varying by specification and rebuild quality. Used units can be found from £3,000–£5,000, but provenance is critical. Fitting adds £1,000–£2,000 depending on labour rates.

items.question: Is the 368DT (3.6 TDV8) a reliable engine?
items.answer: The 368DT has a poor reputation (3.0★). While it offers strong V8 torque and refinement, the early engines (2006–2009) are known for timing chain tensioner failure and, in more severe cases, crankshaft fracture. It is not the reliable 4.4 TDV8 (448DT) that replaced it.

items.question: What is the most common 368DT failure?
items.answer: Timing chain tensioner failure is the most critical on early (2006–2009) engines — causing chain slack, timing drift, and catastrophic valve/piston interference. Turbo actuator failure is also common (typically sticky vanes). The most severe risk is crankshaft fracture, which can occur without warning and writes off the engine.

items.question: Which Land Rover models use the 368DT?
items.answer: The 368DT powered the Range Rover L322 TDV8 from 2006 to 2009 and the Range Rover Sport L320 TDV8 from 2006 to 2012.

items.question: How long does the 368DT (3.6 TDV8) last?
items.answer: With meticulous maintenance — regular oil changes with correct spec, early timing chain tensioner replacement on pre‑2009 examples, and attentive listening for cold‑start rattle — 368DT engines can reach 150,000+ miles. However, many fail well before this due to the known issues. Later (2009+) examples are more durable but still require careful maintenance.
